waitall.__returns: This function returns no value when waiting for all tasks, or a table containing the results of the completed tasks. Each entry is a table with the returned values of the corresponding Task in order
waitall.__example:
-- Create 2 tasks that return multiple values
local t1 = sys.Task(function()
    sleep(500)
    return "alpha", 1
end)

local t2 = sys.Task(function()
    sleep(800)
    return "beta", 2
end)

-- start tasks
t1(); t2()

-- Each entry in results is a table with the returned values of the corresponding Task
local results = waitall(t1, t2)

-- print using table.unpack to expand each Task's return values
for i, res in ipairs(results) do
    local value1, value2 = table.unpack(res)
    print(("Task %d results: %s, %s"):format(i, value1, value2))
end

__params.__description:
Optional Task

objects to wait for. If no Task is provided, the function will wait for all running tasks to terminate.

waitall.__description: Suspend the current execution flow until all running tasks (or only the specified ones) have been terminated.

each.__returns: This function returns an iterator function, that when called (or better inside a for..do loop) will get the next value from the iterable variable.
each.__example:
list = { 3, "apple", "banana", sys.Directory(".") }

-- outputs all values in the list
for item in each(list) do
	print(item)
end

__params.__description: A table, an Object, or a function to iterate on.

each.__description: Returns an iterator function that iterates over the values of a table or Object.
each.__tinydesc: Provide a value iterator for tables/objects

__description: The base module contains global functions specific to Lua<sup>rt</sup>
__tinydesc: Contains all global functions specific to Lua<sup>rt</sup>

is.__returns: This function returns true if the variable inherits from the specified Object, or false otherwise.
is.__example:
Polygon = Object {      -- Defines a new Object Polygon
	sides = 0			-- Defines a 'sides' field in Polygon
}
Square = Object (Polygon) -- Defines a new Object Square, inheriting from Polygon

-- outputs true
print(is(Square, Polygon))

-- outputs false
print(is('a string', Polygon))

-- outputs true
print(is(Square(), Polygon))

__params.__description: A variable for which we want to check inheritance. That variable can be of any type.
An Object or table to check inheritance against.

is.__description: Check if the variable inherits from the specified Object.

type.__returns:
Returns the type of its only argument, as a string. The possible results of this function are "nil", "number", "string", "boolean", "table", "function", "thread", "userdata", "Object" or the name of the last inherited Object.

When using the Luart compatibility interpreter, checking Objects type will return "table".
type.__example:
Polygon = Object {
	sides = 0
}
square = Polygon()
square.sides = 4

-- outputs "Polygon"
print(type(square))

-- outputs "Object"
print(type(Polygon))
type.__description: Returns the type of a variable.
type.__tinydesc: Get the type of a value

super.__returns:
This function returns the ancestor object, or nil if no ancestor is found.
If you want to call ancestor methods with the super() function, you will need to use the "." dot notation to specify the self instance explicitly
Method call using ":" semicolon is not possible as super() returns an Object, not an instance value.
super.__example:
Polygon = Object {}      -- Defines a new Object Polygon

-- Defines a constructor for the Polygon Object
function Polygon:constructor(n)
  -- Defines a new filed 'sides'
  self.sides = n
end

Square = Object (Polygon) -- Defines a new Object Square, inheriting from Polygon

-- Defines a constructor for the Square Object (with 4 sides)
function Square:constructor()
    -- Don't rewrite previous code, just use super() to call the Polygon constructor
  -- to initialize the sides field with the value of 4
    super(self).constructor(self, 4)
end

-- Should print true
print(super(Square) == Polygon)

-- Should print 4
print(Square().sides)

__params.__description: An object (as defined by the Object() function) or an instance.

super.__description:
Get the ancestor of an object or instance.
The super() function permits you to access ancestor methods, fields and properties.
super.__tinydesc: Get the ancestor of an object/instance.

Object.__returns: Returns the new defined object type.

__params.__description: A table or an other previously declared object that serves as a prototype to define a new custom type.

One or more optional tables that contains methods, to extend the object capabilities.

A mixin can also be viewed as an interface with implemented methods.

Object.__description:
Declare a new Object type using the from prototype.
The Object() function implements multilevel single inheritance object oriented programming for Lua.

sleep.__example:
-- Suspend the program for 5 seconds
sleep(5000)

__params.__description: The delay for which current execution flow will be suspended, in milliseconds. Delay is optional, with a default 1 millisecond program suspension.

sleep.__description: Suspend the current execution flow until the delay elapse, allowing tasks to run during this time.

await.__returns: This function returns the Task return values.
await.__example:
-- Counts from 5 to 10
print (await (function (from, to)
    local i = from
    while i <= to do
        sleep(500)
        print(i)
        i = i +1
    end
    -- The Task will return the string "Done !"
    return "Done !"
end, 5, 10))

__params.__description:
A function or a Task object instance to wait for.If a function is supplied, a new task instance is transparently created and started.

Other provided optional arguments are used as function arguments when the Task is started.

await.__description: Wait for the task to terminate, suspending execution and allowing other tasks to run during this time.
await.__tinydesc: Run a Task and wait until it terminates

async.__returns: This function returns the Task object instance.
async.__example:
-- Throws an asynchronous Task which will count from 5 to 10
async (function (from, to)
    local i = from
    while i <= to do
        sleep(500)
        print(i)
        i = i +1
    end
    -- The Task will return the string "Done !"
    return "Done !"
end, 5, 10).after = function(result)
    print(result)
end

-- wait for all task to terminate
waitall()

__params.__description:
A function or a Task object instance to wait for.If a function is supplied, a new task instance is transparently created and started.

Other provided optional arguments are used as function arguments when the Task is started.
